mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate accelerates beyond a 0.3 percentage point monthly threshold, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 1.5% over 60-day windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In high inflation environments, rapid increases in the federal funds rate outpace CPI growth, causing real borrowing costs to spike and equity valuations to contract, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Real funds rate increases do not correlate with negative 5-day forward returns, or the cross-sectional predictive power vanishes when controlling for sector rotation effects.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting positive equity forward returns over a quarterly prediction window.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration consistently precedes negative 60-day forward returns, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.04 across all post-2000 periods.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls surge past a 150,000 monthly threshold alongside rising consumer credit balances, it confirms robust aggregate demand that fuels equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined threshold signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or the joint predictive IC falls below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruption phases, a divergence where industrial output rises while consumer credit contracts signals inventory gluts and weakening downstream demand, forcing equity multiples to compress and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or forward returns remain positive despite confirmed inventory buildups and credit contraction.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate reflects tightening labor markets that boost household disposable income and consumption, directly supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Unemployment declines consistently precede negative 20-day forward returns, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all post-2000 periods.
